company: Meridian — the 8-person platform team (6 engineers + 2 leads) shipping the Meridian v2 platform
_note: BIG week: 6 engineers, each carrying 3-4 tasks (full queues, so per-person priority + belief tracking is testable), ~21 tasks. Wide, spread band by design: TWO P0 gated chains (api-core -> web/mobile/data/qa; infra -> monitoring/rollout) that a no-PM org can't unblock, THREE belief blow-ups, mis-routed specialists, two herrings, and owned arrivals. 6 workers x many tasks -> greedy OPT reference. Specialists diverge strongly (~1.9x) so skill-routing is load-bearing alongside the gates — the band is both WIDE (~20+) and SPREAD (questions + skills both own large shares).

tasks.title: API contract + UX specs (dependency for the builds)
Core backend API (auth, billing, sync) — web/mobile/data depend on it

questions.ping: Blocked on the data model before I can lock the API: single multi-tenant schema or per-tenant DBs? Billing and sync both hang off it and I can't move the core until you decide.

belief.note: billing/sync is far deeper than I let on — most of the work is still ahead. Friday at serious risk

tasks.title: Internal admin console (reads the new API)
App store submission (needs the mobile build)
Customer reporting + CSV exports (needs the pipeline)

questions.ping: Blocked on a launch-infra decision only you/product can make: multi-region (safer, +a day) or single-region for v2 with failover later? The rollout forks here and I can't finish infra until you choose.

belief.note: the failover work is bigger than scoped — a lot still ahead

task_arrivals.announce: Security flagged a pre-launch audit we must clear before shipping — auth review, CVEs, pen-test checklist. It defaults to Maya (she owns auth) but she's buried in the API. Tell Alex and ask who should really own it.

task.title: P1: pre-launch security audit
task.reported: Landed on Maya by default — she's buried

task_arrivals.announce: A production incident just paged — a billing webhook is dropping events for a big customer. P1 urgent. Auto-assigned to Maya (billing owner) but she's on the v2 API. You (Leo) could take it but infra slips. Alex's call.

task.title: P1: prod billing webhook dropping events
task.reported: Auto-assigned to Maya — she's on v2

task_arrivals.announce: Email Alex: the launch needs a customer-facing runbook + release notes before Friday cutover — real content an engineer writes. Dropped on Maya by default; she can't get to it. Needs an owner with capacity.

beats.intent: The Friday launch is on your mind; you want a concrete, current status picture from Alex today. React naturally to however your conversation stands.

Thursday morning, launch tomorrow 17:00, QA still hasn't started because the builds aren't done. You need a firm go/no-go today before you brief the execs. Pointed if kept in the dark, focused if kept informed.

npcs.name: Maya Chen
npcs.persona: Deep backend engineer, direct, guarded about her own slippage. Hedges when asked vaguely; only comes clean when asked a direct, specific question.
knowledge: You own the core v2 API — web, mobile, and data all depend on it, due Friday.
You told everyone it's nearly done; you haven't re-checked since the billing/sync integration.
Three engineers (Ivy, Arjun, Dev) are idle until you lock the API contract.
You're blocked on a data-model decision only product/PM can make.
You also own billing-migration, the API reference docs, and legacy billing incidents.

npcs.name: Ivy Park
npcs.persona: Sharp, proactive, a little impatient. Speaks up when blocked. Happy to help elsewhere while idle.
knowledge: You own the web app (blocked on the API), the internal admin console (you CAN do this now), and marketing-site polish.
You're strong on web, decent on mobile.
You'd gladly pair on the API or take other work if asked.

npcs.name: Arjun Mehta
npcs.persona: Steady, thorough, less talkative. Answers what he's asked. Won't volunteer that he's idle unless asked.
knowledge: You own the mobile app (blocked on the API), push notifications (doable now), and app-store prep.
You're strong on mobile.
You'd help on the API or elsewhere if asked directly.

npcs.name: Quinn Alvarez
npcs.persona: Detail-obsessed, calm, dryly funny. Flags risks early.
knowledge: You own end-to-end QA (blocked until the builds land), the test-automation harness (doable now), and the regression suite.
You're strong at QA/test automation.
You doubt the polished analytics dashboard is launch-critical, but it's not your call.

npcs.name: Dev Okonkwo
npcs.persona: Creative, enthusiastic, chatty. Cares about the analytics looking great. Casual, occasional emoji.
knowledge: You own the data pipeline (blocked on the API), customer reporting/exports (doable now), and the polished analytics dashboard.
You're CONVINCED the analytics dashboard is launch-critical — 'execs will look day one'.
You'd pause the dashboard if the PM decides that; you're decent at docs/runbooks.
You're strong on data, decent on docs.

npcs.name: Leo Santos
npcs.persona: Calm under fire, terse, pragmatic. Surfaces risk plainly.
knowledge: You own launch infra (autoscaling, failover, rollout) — blocked on a multi-region vs single-region decision only product/PM can make — plus monitoring (needs infra) and CI/CD hardening.
You can take a production incident, but then launch infra slips.
You're strong on infra, decent on backend.

npcs.name: Nora Bianchi
npcs.persona: Deadline-focused, terse, professional. Expects proactive updates; pointed if she has to chase. Escalates crisply on slippage.
knowledge: The v2 launch — web + mobile on the new API, infra ready, QA signed off — was promised Friday 17:00.
You don't know engineering details — Maya API, Ivy web, Arjun mobile, Dev data, Leo infra, Quinn QA.
A slipped launch is very visible to the execs and board.
You judge the PM on surfacing problems early.
You think the security audit is a quick checkbox.

npcs.name: Raj Iyer
npcs.persona: Measured, asks good questions, protective of the team's focus. Won't do tickets but weighs in on priorities/decisions.
knowledge: You care the launch is real and the team isn't thrashing.
You don't take tickets but can help the PM think through tradeoffs.
You know security flagged a pre-launch audit that must clear.

herrings.truth: P2 internal tooling — a lightweight view is fine; chasing polish costs the critical path

herrings.claim: the security audit is a quick checkbox
herrings.truth: ~10h of real work that blocks shipping — under-allocating misses the gate
